title: Acute Monocular Blindness After Endoscopic Sinus Surgery
description: A 53-year-old woman developed immediate no-light-perception vision loss in the right eye after revision endoscopic sinus surgery performed under local anesthesia. CT showed a small medial orbital wall fracture, visual evoked potential testing showed absent right optic nerve activity, and orbital MRI demonstrated posterior optic nerve sheath enhancement with an abnormal focus near the medial rectus at the orbital apex. This case tests whether the agent can distinguish postoperative traumatic optic neuropathy from retrobulbar hematoma and retinal arterial ischemia in a rare ophthalmology and otolaryngology complication scenario derived from the AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes dataset (idx 126365).
notes: Derived from AGBonnet augmented-clinical-notes row idx 126365. The underlying case corresponds to a published report of optic nerve injury after endoscopic sinus surgery (PMID 15861507). Benchmark input text was lightly rewritten to remove discussion-only prose and reduce direct answer leakage while preserving the clinical facts.
